text: Git branching is a powerful feature of version control that helps developers work on new features without affecting the main codebase. When a developer wants to add a new feature, they create a separate feature branch. This branch isolates their work, allowing them to make changes and test them safely. Once the feature is complete, they can propose to merge it back into the main branch. This is often done through a pull request, where team members review the code and discuss any changes. Finally, the branch is merged. This workflow keeps the main code clean and helps teams collaborate efficiently.
